checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. The text present in the provided sections (Agenda, Ethics Statement, Minutes, Goals, and Meeting Dates) matches the source document's content. The 'omitted' message is a structural note from the conversion process, not a hallucination of meeting content.

There is no fabricated contact information. The sections containing contact info (such as the RQI and RAK Academy pages) were omitted entirely in the output, so no contact data exists to be verified for fabrication.

The output explicitly states that 8 sections were omitted. Cross-referencing the source PDF (38 pages) with the output shows that Pages 7-27 and Pages 29-36 are entirely missing. These pages contain the 'RQI T-CPR Program Instructional Overview', the 'Saving Lives Together' brochure, and the 'RAK Academy' training course information. This represents more than 75% of the document pages being absent.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document includes Pages 7-17 (RQI Instructional Overview), Pages 18-27 (Program Brochure), Pages 29-33 (Brochure continued), and Pages 34-36 (RAK Academy flyers).

checks.a_analysis: The 'Curriculum Overview Comparison' table on Page 28 of the source document contains 14 rows of data (comparing features like Training Hours, Certification, Performance Baseline, etc.). The corresponding table in the output JSON contains only one data row ('Cost per Learner, per Year:'), missing approximately 93% of the required data rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison' on Page 28 contains rows: RQI T-CPR Course, Quarterly Continuing Education, Quarterly Simulation Skills..., Quarterly Individual..., T-CPR Training Hours, Certificate..., T-CPR Performance..., Resuscitation Quality..., Designated RQI..., RQI Learning..., FirstWatch..., Cardiac Arrest Case Review..., AHA Responder..., Cost per Learner.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table in section 10 ('RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison') contains only one data row: 'Cost per Learner, per Year: $55 $75 $175'

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Within the non-omitted sections (Agenda, Minutes, Goals), the text content is largely preserved. While Point 10 in the Minutes section is split across multiple paragraphs, the information from both pages 5 and 6 of the source is present.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording and data points in the converted sections (e.g., student counts, dollar amounts, and dates) match the source document faithfully. No changes in meaning were observed.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output follows the source document's flow. No instances of duplicate text blocks (paragraphs or sections) were identified that do not exist in the source.

The output document lacks an H1 heading for the overall document title. Section headers are mapped to H2 levels (e.g., 'Ethics Statement' in Section 2, 'Minutes' in Section 3), effectively skipping the top-level H1 which should be used for the document title.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document title on page 1 is 'North Carolina 911 Board Education Committee Meeting Agenda'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The first heading element encountered is an H2 in Section 2: 'Ethics Statement:'. There is no H1 element in the JSON array.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, beginning with Section 2, element index 0.

checks.a_analysis: There are major structural corruptions in table conversion. On page 28, the comparison matrix table is severely truncated, containing only the header row and one data row, omitting roughly 13 rows of content. Additionally, the meeting agenda on page 1, which uses 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' headers in the source, is flattened into a single paragraph block, losing all tabular structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 28 contains a multi-row table with features like 'RQI T-CPR Course', 'Quarterly Continuing Education', etc. Page 1 contains a tabular agenda with three columns.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table in section 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison' contains only the 'Cost per Learner, per Year:' data row. The Section 1 agenda is a 'paragraph' type containing the text 'Tab Topic Presenter'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 1 (Page 1) and Section 'RQI Telecommunicator CPR Curriculum Overview Comparison' (Page 28).

checks.a_analysis: The output preserves the logical reading order of the source PDF, processing content from top to bottom and page by page correctly.

The numbered list (1-13) and nested bullets (under item 8) on the first page agenda are incorrectly converted into a single large paragraph block. This removes the structural meaning of the list items and their hierarchical relationship.
checks.b_evidence_source: Numbered list 1-13 on page 1, with bulleted sub-items for Buncombe County and Haywood County 911 under item 8.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 1 elements contain a single 'paragraph' element where all agenda items are concatenated as plain text (e.g., '1. Chair Opening Remarks Chuck Greene 2. Welcome...').

checks.a_analysis: Due to several sections being omitted in the provided JSON subset, full verification of contact links (emails/phones) and URLs on pages 17, 34, 35, and 36 is not possible. Within the provided sections, no links are present, but no evidence of broken or fabricated links was found in the available data.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document correctly reflects the visual organization of the source PDF, converting the multi-page document into a single-column responsive layout. The sequence of agendas, minutes, instructional overview, brochure content, and goals matches the source's intent. Information is located in predictable sections relative to the source.

All tables from the source PDF, including the agenda table (p. 1), the meeting attendee tables (p. 3), the vote tables (p. 4, 5, 6), and the large curriculum comparison table (p. 28), are successfully rendered as HTML tables. They maintain clear row and column structures, making the data readable and understandable.

A significant number of meaningful images and infographics from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. While decorative backgrounds are intentionally excluded for accessibility, several functional images are absent: the example certificates and credentials (pages 16, 17, 25, 27), the 'Resuscitation excellence' diagram (page 19), the 38% pie chart infographic (page 20), the US map infographic (page 21), and organizational logos such as the First Watch logo (page 29) and the RAK Academy logo (page 34). The absence of these elements constitutes a major loss of visual information provided in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful images including RAK Academy logo (p. 34), First Watch logo (p. 29), example certificates and credentials (p. 16, 17, 25, 27), diagrams for resuscitation excellence (p. 19), and pie chart infographic (p. 20).
checks.b_evidence_output: These meaningful images are entirely missing from the HTML rendering in their respective sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically in sections corresponding to pages 16, 17, 19, 20, 21, 25, 27, 29, and 34.

checks.a_analysis: No instances of content duplication (repeated paragraphs, tables, or sections) were observed in the HTML output.
